item.title: Trump’s Eye Is Already on Cuba
item.description: “Regime change is lined up,” awaiting the president’s signal, according to one administration official.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Salama

item.title: A Friend Gifts Me a Paper Bag of Honeycomb
item.dc:creator: Nina C. Peláez

item.title: The Death of Millennial Feminism
item.description: Lindy West has unwittingly written the obituary for an era.
item.dc:creator: Helen Lewis

item.title: The Provocation ofThe Pitt
item.description: Plus: four new books to look out for, a children’s show for adults, and other culture and entertainment recommendations
item.dc:creator: Josh Tyrangiel

item.title: The Lecture I Couldn’t Give
item.description: Now more than ever, our armed forces need to understand the history of civil-military relations.
item.dc:creator: Kori Schake

item.title: How to Fix DHS
item.description: The answer to the agency’s abuses is not reform; it is wholesale disassembly and restructuring.
item.dc:creator: Paul Rosenzweig

item.title: The First AI Crisis Is Psychological
item.description: The economic shocks may well be coming, but we have already entered an age of profound uncertainty about ourselves and the world around us.
item.dc:creator: Nick Dothée

item.title: Donald Trump Is Nothing Like Robert Mueller
item.description: The absurdity of a man who avoided Vietnam due to “bone spurs” dancing on the grave of a decorated combat veteran
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lemire

item.title: How Does Trump Define Victory in Iran?
item.description: Panelists onWashington Week With The Atlanticjoined to discuss growing opposition to President Trump’s attacks on Iran and what winning a war with unclear objectives could like.
item.dc:creator: The Editors

item.title: Why Some Men Struggle to Keep Up With Friendships
item.description: And how to reimagine those bonds
item.dc:creator: Isabel Fattal

item.title: What a Century-Old Sex Manual Got Right
item.dc:creator: Anna Holmes

item.title: Some Divorce Trends That Aren’t Leaving Your Partner Mid-Hike
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Petri

item.title: The Trouble With Seizing Kharg Island
item.description: The Trump administration is contemplating a move that could end or escalate the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Brynn Tannehill

item.title: Project Hail MaryShould Be Easier to Root For
item.description: The high-stakes space drama badly wants audiences to loosen up.
item.dc:creator: David Sims

item.title: The State That Decided to Topple a Political Giant
item.description: Phil Berger has been the most powerful person in North Carolina for 15 years. That wasn’t enough to save him from voters’ anger at incumbents and legalized gambling.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: Mike Krzyzewski’s Final Insult to Duke’s Haters
item.description: The winningest coach in NCAA men’s basketball history picked a very good successor.
item.dc:creator: Sally Jenkins

item.title: The Disney Princess Who Wasn’t
item.description: The Bachelorettepromoted Taylor Frankie Paul as a new kind of heroine. Then reality got in the way.
item.dc:creator: Megan Garber

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: A World Record
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: What Very Different Places Have in Common
item.description: Gary Shteyngart finds the universal in Cape Town.
item.dc:creator: Boris Kachka

item.title: The Hostile Corporate Takeover of an Entire Country
item.description: What Trump wrought in Venezuela could have come from a Wall Street playbook.
item.dc:creator: Gisela Salim-Peyer
